channel.title: Ad News Feed
channel.description: 広告プラットフォーム最新情報の自動収集フィード

item.title: [General] Boost Google Ranking: Unlock the Power of Reviews! #shorts
item.description: Reviews matter more than you think! Google favors businesses with a high quantity of recent, 5-star reviews, especially those with photos and videos. Local guides and detailed feedback boost your ranking. Make your reviews count! #GoogleBusinessProfile #LocalSEO #OnlineReviews #MarketingTips

item.title: [General] Local SEO Scan: Ranking Google Business Profiles for Services #shorts
item.description: Discover how to find the best local flooring installers. Google Business Profiles scan reveals top-ranked companies, prioritizing proximity and review scores for your home. #LocalSEO #GoogleBusinessProfile #FlooringInstallers #HomeImprovement

item.title: [General] YouTube Tests Variable Thumbnail Sizes on Mobile: What Creators Need to Know About Cropping, CTR, and Thumbnail Design
item.description: YouTube is testing a change that could affect one of the most important assets in a creator’s publishing workflow: the thumbnail. For years, creators, brands, publishers, and agencies have built thumbnail systems around a simple assumption. A video thumbnail would appear inside a mostly predictab

item.title: [General] cPanel Vulnerability CVE-2026-41940: What Active Exploitation Means for Website Owners, Hosting Providers, and Resellers
item.description: A critical cPanel and WHM vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-41940, has moved from vendor bulletin to active exploitation. That shift matters because cPanel is not a niche utility. It is one of the most widely deployed hosting control panels on the internet, used to manage websites, email, databa

item.title: [Google Ads] Google AI Brief for AI Max: What It Is, How It Works, Rollout Details, and What Advertisers Should Do Now
item.description: Google has launched AI Brief for AI Max, adding a new layer of advertiser control to automated Search campaign management. For paid media teams, this is an important development because it changes how campaign intent can be communicated to Google Ads. Instead of relying only on keywords, asset se

item.title: [General] Chivas Regal 16 x Charles Leclerc : The SE!ZE Board Game, Baby Teeth, Somesuch, and the Strategy Behind the Launch
item.description: When a whisky brand works with a Formula 1 driver, the expected result is usually straightforward: a bottle shot, a campaign film, a few social posts, and a limited-edition release designed to ride the attention cycle around a race weekend. That formula still appears often because it is familiar,

item.title: [General] S.Pellegrino Dinner Dialogues With Lewis Hamilton and Ogilvy: Campaign Breakdown, Bottle Strategy, and the Global Rollout
item.description: S.Pellegrino’s new “Dinner Dialogues” campaign is built on a simple idea with broad reach: better conversations can start with better prompts, and a brand can help make that happen if it earns a place at the table. Fronted by Lewis Hamilton and developed with Ogilvy, the campaign turns the brand’

item.title: [General] TikTok Campus Hub : How College Feed, Campus Groups, and Campus Verification Work at 6,000 U.S. Universities
item.description: College social life has always been shaped by whatever communication tool students already use every day. First it was email lists, then Facebook, then group texts, then Discord servers, Instagram DMs, and campus-specific apps. TikTok has now made its own move into that space with Campus Hub, a f

item.title: [Meta Ads] Meta Ads AI Connectors Explained: What Advertisers Can Do With ChatGPT, Claude, and Meta’s MCP Server
item.description: Meta has opened a new path for advertisers that want to work inside AI tools they already use instead of staying inside Ads Manager for every task. Its new Meta Ads AI connectors let businesses connect supported external AI assistants to their Meta ad accounts so they can analyze performance, cre

item.title: [General] LinkedIn Revenue Rose 12% in the First Quarter: What Microsoft’s Latest Results Mean for B2B Marketing, Hiring, and Sales
item.description: LinkedIn’s latest quarter tells a bigger story than a simple revenue increase. Yes, the headline number matters: revenue rose 12% year over year in the quarter tied to the first calendar quarter of 2026. But the more useful takeaway is what sits behind that figure. The platform is becoming more i

item.title: [General] X AI-Powered Ad Platform : What Changed in Ads Manager, How Targeting Works, and What Advertisers Should Watch
item.description: X has begun rolling out a rebuilt advertising platform that the company says is powered by newer AI-driven retrieval and ranking systems. On the surface, that sounds like the kind of platform update every major ad business now talks about. But the significance here is not just that X added more A

item.title: [General] Instagram’s Original Content Algorithm Update: What Changed, What Counts as Original, and What Creators and Brands Should Do Next
item.description: Instagram has expanded its effort to reward original creators and reduce the recommendation advantage long held by repost-heavy accounts. That matters for individual creators, media brands, ecommerce teams, agencies, and anyone using Instagram as a discovery channel. For years, the platform’s big

item.title: [Google Ads] Have you tried AI Max yet?
item.description:
Everyone’s talking about AI Max for Google Ads…
Here’s the honest truth from someone who’s actually tested it.
No meaningful results (yet) And the expectation of seeing any real impact? We’re probably talking 6 months - 2 two years away minimum. (like when PMAX was launched in 2022)
Even for eco

item.title: [Google Ads] Double Your CTR! Every Google Ads Asset Explained [Inside Google Ads Episode 118]
item.description: If you're still running search ads with basic headlines and descriptions, you are firmly average. And in 2026, average just doesn't cut it anymore! In episode 118 of the Inside Google Ads podcast, Google Ads Coach Jyll Saskin Gales walks you through all 15 types of Google Ads assets (formerly known

item.title: [Google Ads] Google Ads Broad Match: The Competitive Advantage Explained #shorts
item.description: Unlock the power of Google Ads! Discover why broad match, with a higher budget, can offer a long-term competitive edge despite initial spend. Learn how exact match and its synonyms are continuously expanding. #GoogleAds #DigitalMarketing #SEO #PPC #AdvertisingTips

item.title: [Google Ads] New: Task Assistant in Google Analytics – Check Your Setup
item.description: Learn how to use Google Analytics' Task Assistant to make sure your property is set up correctly and you're not missing any important features or integrations. Task Assistant is a built-in checklist that shows you exactly what needs to be configured in your Google Analytics property, and it automati

item.title: [Google Ads] Adapt your Shopping campaigns to modern Search with AI Max.
item.description: Built for the next generation of Search, AI Max for Shopping campaigns helps retailers reach shoppers the moment discovery begins.

item.title: [Google Ads] Meet travelers in the moments that matter with Search Campaigns for Travel.
item.description: Upgrade to AI Max to simplify your workflow and access our most advanced tools.

item.title: [Google Ads] AI Max Turns 1 with new ways to steer performance and expansion to more advertisers
item.description: As AI Max turns 1, we’re helping you capture even more opportunities in the expanding Search universe.

item.title: [General] 縦型動画広告の成果につながる冒頭設計とは？BtoC・BtoBの添削事例から学ぶ改善のヒント
item.description:
「なんとなくイケてない気がするけど、どこをどう直せばいいか分からない」 縦型動画広告の制作で、一度はこう感じたことはないでしょうか。成果の差が最も出やすいのが、動画の冒頭数秒の設計です。 本セミナーで…

item.title: [Meta Ads] Meta Ads AI Connectors, App Usage Drops, and More
[General] Adobe Completes Semrush Acquisition: What It Means for SEO, GEO, AI Search Visibility, and Brand Discovery
item.description: Adobe has completed its acquisition of Semrush, closing a deal that moves one of the best-known names in search intelligence into one of the largest customer experience and content ecosystems in enterprise software. On the surface, this looks like a classic platform acquisition: Adobe adds anothe

item.title: [General] Nick Jonas Joins Schick’s “Do Right By Your Skin” Campaign
item.description: If a lot of people briefly thought Nick Jonas was about to launch a skincare line, that was the point. Schick’s “Do Right By Your Skin” campaign arrived with a simple but carefully staged idea: get consumers to look at a razor the way they look at skincare. Instead of talking about shaving as a [

item.title: [General] The 3-Layer Content Framework: How to Align Brand Truth, Audience Relevance, and Channel Fit
item.description: Every strong piece of content does three jobs at once. It represents the company accurately. It matters to the audience immediately. And it matches the environment where people will encounter it. Most content misses at least one of those jobs. Some posts are on-brand but generic. They sound polis

item.title: [General] Why Channel-by-Channel Marketing Is Dying: What the Data Shows and What to Do Instead
item.description: There was a time when channel-by-channel marketing felt sensible. You had a search team, an email team, a social team, maybe a paid media team and a web team. Each group had its own calendar, budget, reports, and goals. If performance looked healthy inside each channel dashboard, leadership assum

item.title: [General] From Bot to Buyer: How Conversational AI Turns Website Conversations Into Qualified Sales 24/7
item.description: A missed lead rarely announces itself. It shows up as a visitor who lands on your pricing page after business hours, asks one or two important questions, gets no useful answer, and leaves. It looks like a buyer who is ready to book a demo but meets a generic form instead of a timely conversation.

item.title: [Google Ads] Google Retires 4 Legacy Ad Policies: What the Streamline Means for Your Campaigns
item.description: Google has discontinued several legacy ad format policies, and while this is not the kind of change that should trigger panic in an active Google Ads account, it is the kind of change that smart advertisers should pay attention to. On the surface, the update looks administrative. Google removed p

item.title: [General] True Value’s Official Sponsor of Grass Campaign: Full Breakdown of the Spring Strategy, SB Mowing Partnership, and Retail Impact
item.description: Every spring, lawn care advertising starts to look familiar. Retailers talk about greener grass, stronger roots, better fertilizer, faster weed control, and the tools homeowners need to get the yard back in shape after winter. The products matter, of course, but the marketing often blends togethe

item.title: [General] YouTube TV Multiview Is Rolling Out to More Subscribers: What Changed, How It Means, and How to Use Custom Multiview
item.description: YouTube TV’s multiview feature has moved from being a useful but limited option into something much closer to what subscribers had been asking for since the feature first appeared. More users are now getting access to a broader multiview experience, and the change is not just about seeing multipl

item.title: [General] Vine Is Back as diVine: What the Comeback Means, How the App Works, and Whether 6-Second Video Still Fits Today
item.description: Vine is back, but not in the way many people assume. The original Vine app is not being restored by X, and this is not simply a nostalgia project that replays a few old clips for longtime internet users. What has returned is a new app called diVine, a six-second looping video platform built to re

item.title: [General] Meta Q1 2026: Daily Active People Fell to 3.56 Billion While Revenue Reached $56.31 Billion
item.description: For years, Meta’s scale story has seemed almost untouchable. The company’s family of apps has operated with such reach, such habitual use, and such global depth that even small fluctuations in user activity attracted outsized attention. That is why Meta’s first-quarter 2026 report stood out immed

item.description: Today's question is about the recommended structure and access levels between a client and agency when onboarding. item.title: [Google Ads] Where to Find the Google Ads Tag (and Install It with GTM)
item.description: Learn how to find the Google Ads Tag and install it on your website using Google Tag Manager. The Google Ads Tag was previously found under Audience Manager, but it has moved, in this tutorial I'll show you exactly where to find it now, how to copy your tag ID, and how to add it to your website usin

item.title: [Meta Ads] Introducing Ads CLI
item.description: Today we're introducing the Meta ads CLI, a command-line tool for developers to manage Meta ad campaigns. Developers and AI agents working with the Meta Marketing API can now create, edit, and analyze campaigns directly from the command line, without writing custom code.

item.description:

item.title: [Google Ads] The End of Manual Bidding Levers: How to Win With Smart Bidding, Performance Max, and Value-Based PPC
item.description: For most of paid media’s history, advertisers won by managing dozens of small controls better than the next account. They set keyword bids by hand. They split campaigns by match type. They adjusted bids by device, geography, hour, audience, and network. They watched search terms closely, pushed s

item.title: [General] The Rise of AI Purchasing Agents: 12 Practical Ways to Optimize for Non-Human Buyers
item.description: If your team still assumes every buyer journey begins with a human reading your homepage, comparing a few pages, and filling out a form, that assumption is aging fast. A growing share of discovery, comparison, and shortlisting is now mediated by AI systems. In consumer contexts, that may look lik

item.title: [General] Brand Trust Is Now a Performance Metric, Not Just a Brand Metric: How to Measure It, Prove ROI, and Turn Trust Into Growth
item.description: There was a time when brand trust lived mostly in the “soft metrics” bucket. It sat next to awareness, recall, favorability, and share of voice. Important, yes. Commercially useful, probably. But often treated as something separate from the hard numbers that executives actually used to run the bu

item.title: [General] Answer Engine Optimization in 2026: A Practical Playbook for Google AI Overviews, ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Claude
item.description: Search is no longer just a list of blue links. item.title: [General] Should you use AI Max?
item.description:
My honest take on AI Max for Search campaigns (recorded April 2026):
I think it has potential. But right now, I’m not prepared to put my name behind it. And here’s the thing most people aren’t saying out loud: the vast majority of businesses don’t need AI Max yet.
You can scale profitably with thi

item.title: [Meta Ads] Why Meta Keeps Pushing Value Rules
item.description: Meta has spent years removing advertiser control over targeting and placements, so actively encouraging value rules seemed contradictory. But it makes sense when you see where things are headed. Jon explains how a new test version lets you bid based on custom audience labels for lead quality and cus

item.description: Supercharge performance across the shopper journey by connecting Albertsons data with Google’s AI and scale.

item.title: [General] Top AI Transcription Tools for Meetings in 2026: 11 Best Options Compared for Accuracy, Notes, and Security
item.description: Meetings create decisions, commitments, risks, and next steps. The problem is that most of that value disappears quickly when notes are incomplete, action items are not captured clearly, or the only record of what happened lives in one person’s memory. That is why AI transcription tools for meeti

item.title: [General] How to Use Zapier: A Step-by-Step Guide to Automating Workflows With AI, Email, CRM, and Jasper
item.description: If you want the short version, here it is: Zapier is a no-code automation platform that connects the tools your team already uses so work moves automatically from one app to another. Instead of copying information from a form into a spreadsheet, sending a Slack alert, creating a CRM contact, and

item.title: [General] Top AI Image Generation Tools in 2026: Best AI Image Generators Compared for Quality, Text Accuracy, Editing, and Commercial Use

The real question is which tool fits the job, the workflow, the brand standard, and the legal

item.title: [Google Ads] How Voice Search Is Changing Google Ads Search Term Reports in 2026: 12 Practical Fixes to Cut Waste and Improve Intent Matching
item.description: If you manage Google Ads closely, you can see the difference immediately in the search term report. The old pattern was compact and predictable. People typed short, compressed phrases like “emergency plumber,” “crm software pricing,” or “best running shoes.” Today, more queries arrive in the form

item.title: [LinkedIn Ads] LinkedIn Advertising: 21 Advanced Tactics to Improve Lead Quality, Lower CPL, and Scale B2B Pipeline
item.description: LinkedIn advertising rewards precision more than volume. That is why it keeps producing very different outcomes for companies that appear, on the surface, to be using the same platform. One team launches broad campaigns, targets a few job titles, adds a form, and calls the channel expensive. Anot

item.title: [General] Ubersuggest Review and Guide (Updated April 2026): Features, Pricing, Accuracy, Pros, Cons, and Best Uses

item.title: [General] Connected TV Advertising in 2026: How CTV Works, What It Costs, and How to Measure Results
item.description: Connected TV advertising has moved from an experimental media line to a standard part of modern video planning. For many brands, that shift did not happen because CTV was new. It happened because audience behavior changed first. People now watch a large share of TV through streaming environments

item.title: [General] Dominate Local SEO: The Ultimate Pest Control Keyword Strategy #shorts

item.title: [General] Consolidate Ads with the New Creative Workflow
[General] White Label SEO Client Retention: 17 Early Warning Signals, Proactive Check-Ins, and a Practical Churn Prevention Playbook
item.description: Client retention in white label SEO is rarely lost in one dramatic moment. Most accounts do not disappear because of a single bad month, one missed keyword target, or one awkward meeting. They leave because several small problems go unaddressed at the same time: expectations start to drift, commu

item.title: [General] Gatorade Brings Back “Is It In You?”: Inside the “Trust What’s Inside” Football Campaign With Pulisic, Vinícius Júnior and Julián Álvarez
item.description: Gatorade’s new global football campaign does two things at once. It revives one of the most recognizable lines in sports marketing, “Is It In You?”, and it translates that legacy for a younger generation of athletes through a very current football lens. The campaign, titled “Trust What’s Inside,”

item.title: [General] White Label SEO for Franchises: A Practical Guide to Location Page Architecture, Brand Consistency, and Scalable Citation Management
item.description: Franchise SEO looks straightforward from the outside. A brand has one name, one broad service set, and multiple locations. In practice, it is one of the most operationally complex forms of local search. Every location needs to be discoverable in its own market, but every location also has to rein

item.title: [General] How to Pitch White Label SEO to Existing Web Design and PPC Clients: Scripts, Packages, and an Agency Upsell Playbook
item.description: If you already serve clients through web design or PPC, you are sitting on the easiest SEO pipeline you will ever have. That is not because SEO is an easy sale. It usually is not. It is because trust is already in place. Your clients already know your team. They have already approved invoices. Th
